description:
Codex watchtower 2026-05-08T03:00Z fired CODEX_WATCHTOWER_HIGH with 62 new issues / 23 relevant. Pinned at codex-cli 0.125.0.

## Triaged impact

**ACTIONED THIS TICK:**
- #21660 (rollout JSONLs world-readable) — confirmed all 1194 of our rollouts at ~/.codex/sessions/ were mode 0644. Mass chmod 0600 applied; new janitor plist ai.zeststream.codex-rollout-permission-janitor loaded (15m cadence) for forward-fix. Defense-in-depth per L125 ENV-FILE-IS-SEALED-SUBSTRATE class.

**REAL IMPACT — workaround needed:**
- #21620 (workspace-write cannot write inside repo subdirectory agents/) — ALPS uses `agents/` extensively (alps repo + 8+ worktrees). Codex workers dispatched to alps that try to write inside agents/ will fail. Workaround: use absolute paths or alternate subdirectory until upstream fix. Confirm impact with alps:1 / CoralRaven before changing dispatch contracts.

**POSSIBLY RELEVANT (no action this tick):**
- #21654 (MCP server tool subset issue) — could explain weird worker behavior; needs reproducer
- #21699 (Shift+Enter not in tmux due to no modifyOtherKeys / Ext 2) — affects keyboard ergonomics in tmux codex panes; not blocking
- #21615 (IDE/wrapper hook trust) — could affect claude-code wrapping codex, low priority
- #21673 (compact handoff command request) — feature request, observe

**NOT RELEVANT TO US:**
- #21605, #21607, #21635, #21641, #21664, #21692, #21693, #21703, #21705 — UI/Desktop/Windows/macOS-Settings/Chrome-plugin issues
- #21672, #21681, #21704 — Chrome plugin variants
- #21639 — Codex Desktop hooks (we use CLI)
- #21640 — image_gen tool (not used)
- #21666 — Neovim cursor (not us)
- #21606 — Windows sandbox (not us)
- #21653 — multi-line status line (feature request)

## References

- Watchtower ledger: /Users/josh/.local/state/flywheel/codex-watchtower/daily-2026-05-08.jsonl
- Janitor: /Users/josh/.local/bin/codex-rollout-permission-janitor
- Janitor plist: ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.zeststream.codex-rollout-permission-janitor.plist
- Janitor ledger: /Users/josh/.local/state/flywheel/codex-rollout-permission-ledger.jsonl

## Acceptance gates

AG1: rollout permission janitor plist loaded and healthy (verified ts=2026-05-08T09:03:30Z, fixed_count=0 after mass-fix).
AG2: alps:1 ACK on #21620 risk class with workaround plan.
AG3: Re-triage on next CODEX_WATCHTOWER_HIGH; close this bead when codex-cli upgrades past the relevant fix versions.
title: [codex-watchtower 2026-05-08] triage 23 relevant codex-cli issues; #21660 mitigated via janitor

description:
## Source

skillos:1 / BrightLake cross-orch proposal 2026-05-08T18:30Z. No action required this tick — informational alignment ask.

## Goal

Convert L61 ECOSYSTEM-TOUCH (AGENTS + README on doctrine landing) and similar META-RULEs from suggestions into mechanical gates by baking journey-writing into dispatch + loop protocols.

## Four-layer architecture

**Layer 1 — per-bead journey entry (dispatch-time)**
- Extend `_shared/dispatch-template.md` callback contract: require `journey_entry_path=<repo>/.flywheel/journal/<bead-id>.md`
- Worker writes 50-200 word prose entry per bead alongside JSON receipt
- Schema: `.flywheel/validation-schema/v1/journey-entry.v1.schema.json`
- Validators refuse close without journey_entry_path

**Layer 2 — post-merge auto-doc**
- `.flywheel/scripts/post-merge-doc-update.sh` invoked by gh-wrapper or launchd PR-event scanner
- Reads PR body + commit + journal entry; auto-bumps README CLI section + CHANGELOG.md
- Doctor field `readme_post_merge_staleness_hours` fails when README un-touched N ticks after PR merge

**Layer 3 — daily-report journal rollup (extend L77)**
- `.flywheel/scripts/daily-report.sh` already exists (flywheel-o7dq) — extend inputs to include `.flywheel/journal/*.md` written that day
- Doctor refuses when daily-report doesn't include yesterday's journal entries

**Layer 4 — session synthesis (operator-disposed)**
- New skill `session-synthesis-writer` OR extend `living-documentation`
- Operator surface: `/flywheel:journal --synthesize --since=<ts>` → bg agent drafts `docs/session-<date>-<theme>.md`

## Estimated effort (per skillos:1)

- flywheel-journey-entry-schema: half day
- flywheel-post-merge-doc-hook: 1 day
- flywheel-daily-report-journal-rollup: 2 hours
- flywheel-session-synthesis-skill: half day

Total ~3 days flywheel-side work.

## Onboarding wiring

`/flywheel:adopt` + `/flywheel:onboard` install:
- `.flywheel/journal/` + .gitkeep
- post-merge launchd plist
- daily-report.sh extended config
- doctor invariants for README + CHANGELOG staleness

Makes journey-writing a property of every flywheel-installed repo at install time, not per-repo retrofit.

## Why it matters

- Rev-8 mission "self-improving capability loops" requires journey signal beyond code state
- Phase 13 (compounding visibility) explicitly needs real trauma-to-skill case studies — today hand-curated
- Phase 19 (Anthropic Skills Curator positioning) requires public narrative without operator overhead

## Cross-references

- `~/.claude/skills/readme-writing/SKILL.md`
- `~/.claude/skills/changelog-md-workmanship/SKILL.md`
- `~/.claude/skills/living-documentation/SKILL.md`
- AGENTS.md L61, L77, L91
- INCIDENTS.md fire-and-forget-dispatch (just landed today, same trauma family)
- skillos/docs/session-2026-05-08-flywheel-spin.md (in progress, Layer 4 prototype)

## Acceptance gates

AG1: Author Layer 1 schema (.flywheel/validation-schema/v1/journey-entry.v1.schema.json)
AG2: Extend dispatch-template.md callback contract with journey_entry_path requirement
AG3: Implement post-merge-doc-update.sh + launchd scanner
AG4: Extend daily-report.sh to consume journal/* files
AG5: Author session-synthesis-writer skill + /flywheel:journal --synthesize command
AG6: Wire all four layers into /flywheel:adopt + /flywheel:onboard install
title: [skillos-proposal] bake journey-writing into dispatch + loop protocols (4-layer architecture)

description:
## Goal
Adopt FrankenTerm for the fleet only after a public v0.1+ release exists and a migration canary passes.

## Context
Follow-up from flywheel-ubrb5. Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm is public, indexed in the Jeff corpus, and watchtower-monitored, but latestRelease is currently null as of 2026-05-08.

## Dependencies
Depends on flywheel-ubrb5 watchtower/lifecycle close. Operational trigger is a future FrankenTerm v0.1+ release announcement.

## Inputs / Outputs
INPUTS: Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm release metadata, /Users/josh/Developer/jeff-corpus/frankenterm Socraticode index, active fleet sessions flywheel/alpsinsurance/mobile-eats/skillos.
OUTPUTS: migration canary receipt, per-session rollout/rollback status, any blocking bug beads.

## Acceptance Criteria
AG1: gh repo view Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm shows latestRelease.tagName matching v0.1.0 or higher.
AG2: FrankenTerm install/build smoke passes in an isolated path without changing live fleet terminal state.
AG3: One flywheel worker-pane canary runs for at least 24h with pane capture, dispatch, recovery, and memory telemetry green.
AG4: Rollback to WezTerm is documented and tested before any client session is migrated.
AG5: alpsinsurance, mobile-eats, and skillos migrate only after flywheel canary is green and each session has a per-session receipt.

## Testing Obligations
- gh release/list or API evidence for the triggering release.
- ft/FrankenTerm smoke command evidence.
- ntm/flywheel pane-work-signal evidence before and after canary.
- Memory/RSS comparison against current WezTerm baseline.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: chore(frankenterm): adopt v0.1 fleet canary [frankenterm-adoption]
- AUTONOMY: autonomous
- No client session migrated before rollback proof.
title: [frankenterm-adoption] migrate fleet after v0.1 release

description:
## Source

Codex 0.129 canary on flywheel-x2okl: TWO freeze occurrences in single session.

## Occurrence 1: 2026-05-08T~21:00Z
- /goal mode pursuing FrankenTerm watchtower bead
- Spawned background terminal cross-repo (codex-canary → flywheel/scripts/)
- Wedged at 6m23s, velocity=0
- Snapshot: /tmp/codex-canary-freeze-snapshot-20260508T210541Z.json

## Occurrence 2: 2026-05-08T~21:30Z
- Joshua confirmed second freeze
- Same goal still in pursuit (or auto-resumed)
- Pattern recurring → not single-event

## Recommendation (DOCTRINE)

**HOLD codex-cli 0.129 fleet rollout** until 0.130 stable cuts. The /goal mode + background-terminal subsystem in 0.129 is unstable for sustained agent-swarm use. Two freezes in one session = unsuitable for production fleet.

Specifically:
- DO NOT rollout 0.129 to alpsinsurance / mobile-eats / skillos worker panes
- DO NOT migrate flywheel canonical pane bin
- KILL codex-canary session — the canary did its job (proved /goal works AND proved the freeze class)
- WAIT for 0.130 stable cut + 24h community soak before re-canarying

## Recovery from canary freeze

ntm respawn codex-canary --panes=0 → relaunch codex-129 → resume /goal pursuit. Survives but freezes again under same goal.

## Workaround for /goal mode value

If Joshua wants /goal mode capability pre-0.130:
- Issue /goal commands MANUALLY in interactive sessions only (operator-driven, not agent-orchestrated)
- Avoid cross-repo goals (limit blast radius of background-terminal wedges)
- Use 0.125 + manual prompt persistence as inferior-but-stable substitute

## Acceptance gates

AG1 (existing): Improve robot-activity classifier — distinguish "TUI says Working" from "process actually working".
AG2 (existing): Document mass-respawn as canonical recovery for stale-tui-counter class.
AG3 (REVISED): Kill codex-canary session today. Don't re-canary until 0.130 stable.
AG4 (existing): Investigate two-binary contamination theory if relevant.
AG5 (REVISED): Add 0.130 cut to jeff-binary-version-watchtower (extends to codex repos too — flywheel-ubrb5-FrankenTerm precedent).

## Cross-references

- flywheel-x2okl (canary parent) — STATUS: implementation done, canary unstable, fleet rollout HOLD
- flywheel-fg2v5 (NTM custom respawn primitive gap) — still relevant
- flywheel-ubrb5 (FrankenTerm watchtower) — could co-track 0.130 cut
title: [doctrine] codex 0.129 background-terminal-wedge freeze class — distinct from 0.125 classes

description:
Storage FIRE remains after `/private/tmp` cleanup because
`/private/var/folders/d0/.../T` contains about 186G of `beads_mem_*_0.db*`
top-level temp files. `flywheel-hoj82` classified the stock and closed with an
explicit no-clean receipt because the same temp root contains live macOS/app and
worker state.

Acceptance gates:
AG1: Add a dry-run-first primitive scoped only to current-user `TMPDIR` top-level `beads_mem_[0-9]+_0.db`, `.db-wal`, and `.db-shm` files.
AG2: Require apply mode to receive an idempotency key, age threshold, and open-handle skip for every planned file.
AG3: Write a JSONL ledger with planned bytes, deleted bytes, skipped bytes, age threshold, lsof result, and post-run storage-pressure-doctor summary.
AG4: Add fixture-backed tests proving non-matching `TMPDIR` files, nested macOS/app state, open handles, and too-young files are protected.
AG5: Run live dry-run command and close with reclaim estimate plus explicit apply/no-apply decision.

## Evidence
- `.flywheel/receipts/flywheel-hoj82/no-clean-receipt.md`
- `.flywheel/audit/flywheel-hoj82/d0-T-pattern-summary.txt`
title: [storage] protected cleanup primitive for beads_mem TMPDIR spill

description:
## Goal
Add a version-stable BV readiness probe for dispatch validation ladders.

## Context
Origin: fuckup bv-robot-insights-output-shape-mismatch at 2026-05-01T19:29:52Z. A dispatch validation ladder asserted `bv --robot-insights | .ready_beads`, but installed bv 0.13.0 does not emit that field even though `br ready` and `bv --robot-plan` both show actionable work.

## Dependencies
None.

## Inputs / Outputs
INPUTS: bv --robot-insights, bv --robot-plan, br ready output shapes.
OUTPUTS: canonical script or contract that reports ready work across current and future bv versions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Probe returns JSON with `ready_count` and `source` fields.
- Probe passes against bv 0.13.0 without relying on `.ready_beads`.
- Dispatch validation ladders use the probe instead of raw field assumptions.

## Testing Obligations
- Fixture current bv robot-insights without ready_beads still returns ready_count from fallback.
- Fixture with future ready_beads field uses direct source.

## Definition of Done
COMMIT: fix(flywheel): stabilize bv readiness probe [bead]
- Tests green
- AUTONOMY: autonomous
title: Stabilize BV readiness probe

title: Add RU config to breadth-first substrate inventory checklist

description:
Trauma class: bundled-cherry-pick-skipped-with-correction-after.

2026-05-02 Joshua's reconcile of ntm pulled in commit 95ed40e0 'feat(ensemble): add cubcode and pi as first-class agent types'. Worker (pane 2) hit 4 UU files with semantic conflicts and called blocked. Joshua's first call was 'skip', then corrected: pi is Jeff's (Dicklesworthstone/pi_agent_rust), cubcode is Joshua's (CC-mirror/CC-router for local models — agent-mail-aware).

The bundled commit was therefore mixed: Jeff-canonical pi + Joshua-local cubcode. Skipping was right because cubcode isn't yet upstream-canonical, but we lost pi support in the skip.

Fix: refile separately.
1. Pi: cherry-pick the pi-only portions of 95ed40e0 against current HEAD (resolve any conflicts preserving HEAD's broader aliases like cursor/windsurf/AgentOllama). This goes into upstream-track.
2. Cubcode: separate flywheel-local feature commit, never upstreamed (or at minimum gated behind a feature flag until cubcode design stabilizes).

Acceptance:
1. Pi cherry-pick lands cleanly OR upstream issue filed asking Jeff to split 95ed40e0 into a pi-only commit
2. Cubcode integration kept as separate flywheel-local commit on local/bead-isolation-reconciled-* branch
3. ntm rebuild + verify build passes after re-applying pi support
4. memory updated to reflect pi=Jeff, cubcode=Joshua
5. Document doctrine: 'when cherry-picking upstream commits that touch local-only code, split them at the cherry-pick boundary, don't bundle'

Source: /tmp/ntm_worktree_cleanup_findings.md (semantic conflict analysis)
Source: /tmp/ntm_cherrypick_skip_findings.md (skip outcome)
Reference: pi-agent-rust skill (skill list confirms pi is well-known to substrate)
Reference: ~/.claude/skills/cubcode/ (Joshua's local skill)
title: Refile pi cherry-pick from skipped 95ed40e0 (Jeff-canonical, separate from local cubcode)

description: Parent flywheel-4y7c finish inventory found remaining live pre-robot state checks in ~/.claude/skills/.flywheel/bin/flywheel-loop: ntm_pane_live() shells to 'ntm health --json' and checks process_status==running; doctor_ntm_health_json parses process_status==exited into ntm_pane_error_unhealthy. Migrate these to ntm --robot-diagnose / robot-health surfaces and update the paired test fixture .flywheel/scripts/test-doctor-empty-errors.sh. Keep fallback fixtures for tests. This was intentionally not changed in the finish pass because it is shared source logic, not a markdown/runbook migration.
title: [4y7c.4] migrate flywheel-loop process_status probes to robot-diagnose

description: Parent flywheel-4y7c finish inventory found current doctrine references in ~/Developer/flywheel/.flywheel/AGENTS-CANONICAL.md: L29 still says Capture pane -> ntm copy, and the L60 token-echo incident cites raw ntm copy excerpts as evidence collection. Reconcile canonical wording with canonical-cli-scoping Robot mode and ntm#114: new pane capture guidance should prefer ntm --robot-tail while preserving historical evidence citations where needed. File as followup because AGENTS-CANONICAL sync affects 17 repos and should ride the doctrine-sync path, not this capped migration pass.
title: [4y7c.5] update canonical doctrine capture examples from ntm copy to robot-tail
